topic: NHANES 2017-2018 慢病风险数据分析与文章写作

decisions.decision: Use checkpoint-first ARS workflow. Do not run analysis or draft article until user confirms each stage.
User clarified the output target is an SCI journal manuscript, not a training article or public essay.

Initial literature scan suggests common diabetes risk-factor analyses are too saturated for SCI; prioritize a sharper RQ around undiagnosed dysglycemia, metabolically unhealthy normal weight adults, eGDR, or lifestyle clustering.
User selected the conservative RQ1 route: use current NHANES 2017-2018 data to study HbA1c-defined undiagnosed diabetes among self-reported non-diabetic adults.

User confirmed S3 execution and accepted the five method points: single 2017-2018 cycle, R survey package, fasting glucose as sensitivity analysis, pregnancy exclusion check, prediabetes as supplementary analysis.

R survey analysis completed locally. Key execution issue: full model is parameter-heavy for a single NHANES cycle's design degrees of freedom; interpretation should emphasize CI and consider a parsimonious model in S4/S2 revision.

Interpretation stage recommends a parsimonious regression model or S3b revision before moving to outline, because the current full model is too parameter-heavy for a single-cycle NHANES SCI manuscript.
User confirmed returning to S3b to generate a parsimonious main model and final Table 2 before S5 outline.
S3b completed. Recommended final Table 2 is the waist-circumference parsimonious model, with BMI and categorical screening models as sensitivity analyses.
User confirmed entering S5 outline. Produce IMRaD outline and table/figure plan only; do not draft manuscript text.
User requested a literature source matrix before S6 and set S6 target manuscript length to 6,500-8,500 English words.
Literature matrix created at checkpoints/stage-S5b-literature-matrix.md. Official sources are usable now; several PubMed/PMC candidate studies are marked for S7 DOI/full-text verification.
User confirmed S6. Draft English SCI manuscript at 6,500-8,500 words, allow limited targeted literature supplementation, and leave full citation verification to S7.

User said continue after S6. S7 integrity/citation check completed. Core data values match local outputs, but citation layer is not submission-ready; recommend S7b final reference verification and citation replacement before S8.

User confirmed entering S7b. A citation-clean manuscript draft was created without overwriting S6. Working references were replaced with a verified 17-item Vancouver-style reference list; unverified placeholders were removed or downgraded. Recommend S8 reviewer-style critique next.

User confirmed entering S8. Simulated peer review completed on the S7b citation-clean draft. Editorial decision: Major Revision. Recommended next step is S8b revision implementation, not S9 finalization.

User confirmed entering S8b. Major-revision implementation completed: actual tables and figures generated, fasting-glucose sensitivity recalculated with WTSAF2YR, missingness/model sample details added, targeted equity/screening references inserted, and a revised 7,812-word manuscript created. S9 remains pending until explicit user confirmation.

User confirmed entering S9. A generic SCI submission-preparation package was produced, including final manuscript Markdown/DOCX, figures, tables, STROBE mapping, cover letter draft, response-to-reviewers draft, readiness checklist, package manifest, and closeout report. Target-journal-specific formatting remains a human decision because no journal was specified.

risks: NHANES is a complex survey; formal epidemiologic inference requires weights, strata and PSU handling.
Cross-sectional public-use data cannot support causal claims.
Target reader is now scholarly reviewers/editors, but the research question still requires novelty and feasibility assessment before analysis.
